name.description: The name of the file as it appears in the editor.

role.description: The role of the user making the API request in relation to the file.

lastModified.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the file was last modified.

editorType.description: The type of editor associated with this file.

thumbnailUrl.description: A URL to a thumbnail image of the file.

version.description: The version number of the file. This number is incremented when a file is modified and can be used to check if the file has changed between requests.

components.description: A mapping from component IDs to component metadata.

componentSets.description: A mapping from component set IDs to component set metadata.

schemaVersion.description: The version of the file schema that this file uses.

styles.description: A mapping from style IDs to style metadata.

linkAccess.description: The share permission level of the file link.

mainFileKey.description: The key of the main file for this file. If present, this file is a component or component set.

branches.description: A list of branches for this file.

thumbnail_url.description: A URL to a thumbnail image of the branch.

last_modified.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the branch was last modified.

id.description: A string uniquely identifying this node within the document.

name.description: The name given to the node by the user in the tool.

type.description: The type of the node

visible.description: Whether or not the node is visible on the canvas.

locked.description: If true, layer is locked and cannot be edited

isFixed.description: Whether the layer is fixed while the parent is scrolling

scrollBehavior.description: How layer should be treated when the frame is resized

rotation.description: The rotation of the node, if not 0.

componentPropertyReferences.description: A mapping of a layer's property to component property name of component properties attached to this node. The component property name can be used to look up more information on the corresponding component's or component set's componentPropertyDefinitions.

pluginData.description: Data written by plugins that is visible only to the plugin that wrote it. Requires the `pluginData` to include the ID of the plugin.

sharedPluginData.description: Data written by plugins that is visible to all plugins. Requires the `pluginData` parameter to include the string "shared".

boundVariables.description: A mapping of field to the variables applied to this field. Most fields will only map to a single `VariableAlias`. However, for properties like `fills`, `strokes`, `size`, `componentProperties`, and `textRangeFills`, it is possible to have multiple variables bound to the field.

explicitVariableModes.description: A mapping of variable collection ID to mode ID representing the explicitly set modes for this node.

children.description: An array of nodes that are direct children of this node

absoluteBoundingBox.description: Bounding box of the node in absolute space coordinates.

absoluteRenderBounds.description: The actual bounds of a node accounting for drop shadows, thick strokes, and anything else that may fall outside the node's regular bounding box defined in `x`, `y`, `width`, and `height`. The `x` and `y` inside this property represent the absolute position of the node on the page. This value will be `null` if the node is invisible.

preserveRatio.description: Keep height and width constrained to same ratio.

constraints.description: Horizontal and vertical layout constraints for node.

relativeTransform.description: The top two rows of a matrix that represents the 2D transform of this node relative to its parent. The bottom row of the matrix is implicitly always (0, 0, 1). Use to transform coordinates in geometry. Only present if `geometry=paths` is passed.

size.description: Width and height of element. This is different from the width and height of the bounding box in that the absolute bounding box represents the element after scaling and rotation. Only present if `geometry=paths` is passed.

layoutAlign.description:
Determines if the layer should stretch along the parent's counter axis. This property is only provided for direct children of auto-layout frames.

- `INHERIT`
- `STRETCH`

In previous versions of auto layout, determined how the layer is aligned inside an auto-layout frame. This property is only provided for direct children of auto-layout frames.

- `MIN`
- `CENTER`
- `MAX`
- `STRETCH`

In horizontal auto-layout frames, "MIN" and "MAX" correspond to "TOP" and "BOTTOM". In vertical auto-layout frames, "MIN" and "MAX" correspond to "LEFT" and "RIGHT".

layoutGrow.description: This property is applicable only for direct children of auto-layout frames, ignored otherwise. Determines whether a layer should stretch along the parent's primary axis. A `0` corresponds to a fixed size and `1` corresponds to stretch.

layoutPositioning.description: Determines whether a layer's size and position should be determined by auto-layout settings or manually adjustable.

minWidth.description: The minimum width of the frame. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames or direct children of auto-layout frames.

maxWidth.description: The maximum width of the frame. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames or direct children of auto-layout frames.

minHeight.description: The minimum height of the frame. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames or direct children of auto-layout frames.

maxHeight.description: The maximum height of the frame. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames or direct children of auto-layout frames.

layoutSizingHorizontal.description:
The horizontal sizing setting on this auto-layout frame or frame child.
- `FIXED`
- `HUG`: only valid on auto-layout frames and text nodes
- `FILL`: only valid on auto-layout frame children

layoutSizingVertical.description:
The vertical sizing setting on this auto-layout frame or frame child.
- `FIXED`
- `HUG`: only valid on auto-layout frames and text nodes
- `FILL`: only valid on auto-layout frame children

gridRowCount.description: The number of rows in the grid layout. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridColumnCount.description: The number of columns in the grid layout. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridRowGap.description: The distance between rows in the grid layout. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridColumnGap.description: The distance between columns in the grid layout. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridColumnsSizing.description: The string for the CSS grid-template-columns property. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridRowsSizing.description: The string for the CSS grid-template-rows property. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridChildHorizontalAlign.description: Determines how a GRID frame's child should be aligned in the horizontal direction within its grid area. This property is only applicable for direct children of frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridChildVerticalAlign.description: Determines how a GRID frame's child should be aligned in the vertical direction within its grid area. This property is only applicable for direct children of frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridRowSpan.description: The number of rows that a GRID frame's child should span. This property is only applicable for direct children of frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridColumnSpan.description: The number of columns that a GRID frame's child should span. This property is only applicable for direct children of frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridRowAnchorIndex.description: The index of the row that a GRID frame's child should be anchored to. This property is only applicable for direct children of frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

gridColumnAnchorIndex.description: The index of the column that a GRID frame's child should be anchored to. This property is only applicable for direct children of frames with `layoutMode: "GRID"`.

clipsContent.description: Whether or not this node clip content outside of its bounds

background.description: Background of the node. This is deprecated, as backgrounds for frames are now in the `fills` field.

backgroundColor.description: Background color of the node. This is deprecated, as frames now support more than a solid color as a background. Please use the `fills` field instead.

layoutGrids.description: An array of layout grids attached to this node (see layout grids section for more details). GROUP nodes do not have this attribute

overflowDirection.description: Whether a node has primary axis scrolling, horizontal or vertical.

layoutMode.description: Whether this layer uses auto-layout to position its children.

primaryAxisSizingMode.description: Whether the primary axis has a fixed length (determined by the user) or an automatic length (determined by the layout engine). This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

counterAxisSizingMode.description: Whether the counter axis has a fixed length (determined by the user) or an automatic length (determined by the layout engine). This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

primaryAxisAlignItems.description: Determines how the auto-layout frame's children should be aligned in the primary axis direction. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

counterAxisAlignItems.description: Determines how the auto-layout frame's children should be aligned in the counter axis direction. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

paddingLeft.description: The padding between the left border of the frame and its children. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

paddingRight.description: The padding between the right border of the frame and its children. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

paddingTop.description: The padding between the top border of the frame and its children. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

paddingBottom.description: The padding between the bottom border of the frame and its children. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

itemSpacing.description: The distance between children of the frame. Can be negative. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

itemReverseZIndex.description: Determines the canvas stacking order of layers in this frame. When true, the first layer will be draw on top. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

strokesIncludedInLayout.description: Determines whether strokes are included in layout calculations. When true, auto-layout frames behave like css "box-sizing: border-box". This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames.

layoutWrap.description: Whether this auto-layout frame has wrapping enabled.

counterAxisSpacing.description: The distance between wrapped tracks of an auto-layout frame. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutWrap: "WRAP"`

counterAxisAlignContent.description: Determines how the auto-layout frame’s wrapped tracks should be aligned in the counter axis direction. This property is only applicable for auto-layout frames with `layoutWrap: "WRAP"`.

blendMode.description: How this node blends with nodes behind it in the scene (see blend mode section for more details)

exportSettings.description: An array of export settings representing images to export from the node.

fillOverrideTable.description: Map from ID to PaintOverride for looking up fill overrides. To see which regions are overridden, you must use the `geometry=paths` option. Each path returned may have an `overrideID` which maps to this table.

fillGeometry.description: Only specified if parameter `geometry=paths` is used. An array of paths representing the object fill.

strokeGeometry.description: Only specified if parameter `geometry=paths` is used. An array of paths representing the object stroke.

strokeCap.description: A string enum describing the end caps of vector paths.

strokeMiterAngle.description: Only valid if `strokeJoin` is "MITER". The corner angle, in degrees, below which `strokeJoin` will be set to "BEVEL" to avoid super sharp corners. By default this is 28.96 degrees.

fills.description: An array of fill paints applied to the node.

styles.description: A mapping of a StyleType to style ID (see Style) of styles present on this node. The style ID can be used to look up more information about the style in the top-level styles field.

strokes.description: An array of stroke paints applied to the node.

strokeWeight.description: The weight of strokes on the node.

strokeAlign.description:
Position of stroke relative to vector outline, as a string enum

- `INSIDE`: stroke drawn inside the shape boundary
- `OUTSIDE`: stroke drawn outside the shape boundary
- `CENTER`: stroke drawn centered along the shape boundary

strokeJoin.description: A string enum with value of "MITER", "BEVEL", or "ROUND", describing how corners in vector paths are rendered.

strokeDashes.description:
An array of floating point numbers describing the pattern of dash length and gap lengths that the vector stroke will use when drawn.

For example a value of [1, 2] indicates that the stroke will be drawn with a dash of length 1 followed by a gap of length 2, repeated.

individualStrokeWeights.description: An object including the top, bottom, left, and right stroke weights. Only returned if individual stroke weights are used.

cornerRadius.description: Radius of each corner if a single radius is set for all corners

cornerSmoothing.description: A value that lets you control how "smooth" the corners are. Ranges from 0 to 1. 0 is the default and means that the corner is perfectly circular. A value of 0.6 means the corner matches the iOS 7 "squircle" icon shape. Other values produce various other curves.

rectangleCornerRadii.description:
Array of length 4 of the radius of each corner of the frame, starting in the top left and proceeding clockwise.

Values are given in the order top-left, top-right, bottom-right, bottom-left.

effects.description: An array of effects attached to this node (see effects section for more details)

isMask.description: Does this node mask sibling nodes in front of it?

maskType.description:
If this layer is a mask, this property describes the operation used to mask the layer's siblings. The value may be one of the following:

- ALPHA: the mask node's alpha channel will be used to determine the opacity of each pixel in the masked result.
- VECTOR: if the mask node has visible fill paints, every pixel inside the node's fill regions will be fully visible in the masked result. If the mask has visible stroke paints, every pixel inside the node's stroke regions will be fully visible in the masked result.
- LUMINANCE: the luminance value of each pixel of the mask node will be used to determine the opacity of that pixel in the masked result.

isMaskOutline.description: True if maskType is VECTOR. This field is deprecated; use maskType instead.

componentPropertyDefinitions.description: A mapping of name to `ComponentPropertyDefinition` for every component property on this component. Each property has a type, defaultValue, and other optional values.

characters.description: The raw characters in the text node.

style.description: Style of text including font family and weight.

characterStyleOverrides.description: The array corresponds to characters in the text box, where each element references the 'styleOverrideTable' to apply specific styles to each character. The array's length can be less than or equal to the number of characters due to the removal of trailing zeros. Elements with a value of 0 indicate characters that use the default type style. If the array is shorter than the total number of characters, the characters beyond the array's length also use the default style.

layoutVersion.description: Internal property, preserved for backward compatibility. Avoid using this value.

styleOverrideTable.description: Map from ID to TypeStyle for looking up style overrides.

lineTypes.description:
An array with the same number of elements as lines in the text node, where lines are delimited by newline or paragraph separator characters. Each element in the array corresponds to the list type of a specific line. List types are represented as string enums with one of these possible values:

- `NONE`: Not a list item.
- `ORDERED`: Text is an ordered list (numbered).
- `UNORDERED`: Text is an unordered list (bulleted).

lineIndentations.description: An array with the same number of elements as lines in the text node, where lines are delimited by newline or paragraph separator characters. Each element in the array corresponds to the indentation level of a specific line.

characters.description: The raw characters in the text path node.

style.description: Style of text including font family and weight.

characterStyleOverrides.description: The array corresponds to characters in the text box, where each element references the 'styleOverrideTable' to apply specific styles to each character. The array's length can be less than or equal to the number of characters due to the removal of trailing zeros. Elements with a value of 0 indicate characters that use the default type style. If the array is shorter than the total number of characters, the characters beyond the array's length also use the default style.

layoutVersion.description: Internal property, preserved for backward compatibility. Avoid using this value.

styleOverrideTable.description: Map from ID to TextPathTypeStyle for looking up style overrides.

characters.description: Text contained within a text box.

transitionNodeID.description: Node ID of node to transition to in prototyping

transitionDuration.description: The duration of the prototyping transition on this node (in milliseconds). This will override the default transition duration on the prototype, for this node.

transitionEasing.description: The easing curve used in the prototyping transition on this node.

items.description: An array of the interactions on this node, each containing a trigger and one or more actions.

devStatus.description: Represents whether or not a node has a particular handoff (or dev) status applied to it.

description.description: An optional field where the designer can add more information about the design and what has changed.

prototypeStartNodeID.description: Node ID that corresponds to the start frame for prototypes. This is deprecated with the introduction of multiple flows. Please use the `flowStartingPoints` field.

flowStartingPoints.description: An array of flow starting points sorted by its position in the prototype settings panel.

prototypeDevice.description: The device used to view a prototype.

prototypeBackgrounds.description: The background color of the prototype (currently only supports a single solid color paint).

type.description: The type of this node, represented by the string literal "BOOLEAN_OPERATION"

booleanOperation.description: A string enum indicating the type of boolean operation applied.

type.description: The type of this node, represented by the string literal "SECTION"

sectionContentsHidden.description: Whether the contents of the section are visible

type.description: The type of this node, represented by the string literal "FRAME"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "GROUP"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "COMPONENT"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "COMPONENT_SET"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "VECTOR"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "STAR"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "LINE"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "ELLIPSE"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "REGULAR_POLYGON"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "RECTANGLE"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "TEXT"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "TEXT_PATH"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "TABLE"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "TABLE_CELL"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "TRANSFORM_GROUP"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "SLICE"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "INSTANCE"

componentId.description: ID of component that this instance came from.

isExposedInstance.description: If true, this node has been marked as exposed to its containing component or component set.

exposedInstances.description: IDs of instances that have been exposed to this node's level.

componentProperties.description: A mapping of name to `ComponentProperty` for all component properties on this instance. Each property has a type, value, and other optional values.

overrides.description: An array of all of the fields directly overridden on this instance. Inherited overrides are not included.

type.description: The type of this node, represented by the string literal "EMBED"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "LINK_UNFURL"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "STICKY"

authorVisible.description: If true, author name is visible.

type.description: The type of this node, represented by the string literal "SHAPE_WITH_TEXT"

shapeType.description: Geometric shape type. Most shape types have the same name as their tooltip but there are a few exceptions. ENG_DATABASE: Cylinder, ENG_QUEUE: Horizontal cylinder, ENG_FILE: File, ENG_FOLDER: Folder.

type.description: The type of this node, represented by the string literal "CONNECTOR"

connectorStart.description: The starting point of the connector.

connectorEnd.description: The ending point of the connector.

connectorStartStrokeCap.description: A string enum describing the end cap of the start of the connector.

connectorEndStrokeCap.description: A string enum describing the end cap of the end of the connector.

type.description: The type of this node, represented by the string literal "WASHI_TAPE"
The type of this node, represented by the string literal "WIDGET"

FlowStartingPoint.description: A flow starting point used when launching a prototype to enter Presentation view.

PrototypeDevice.description: The device used to view a prototype.

type.description:
Type of constraint to apply:

- `SCALE`: Scale by `value`.
- `WIDTH`: Scale proportionally and set width to `value`.
- `HEIGHT`: Scale proportionally and set height to `value`.

value.description: See type property for effect of this field.

BlendMode.description:
This type is a string enum with the following possible values

Normal blends:
- `PASS_THROUGH` (only applicable to objects with children)
- `NORMAL`

Darken:
- `DARKEN`
- `MULTIPLY`
- `LINEAR_BURN`
- `COLOR_BURN`

Lighten:
- `LIGHTEN`
- `SCREEN`
- `LINEAR_DODGE`
- `COLOR_DODGE`

Contrast:
- `OVERLAY`
- `SOFT_LIGHT`
- `HARD_LIGHT`

Inversion:
- `DIFFERENCE`
- `EXCLUSION`

Component:
- `HUE`
- `SATURATION`
- `COLOR`
- `LUMINOSITY`

ColorStop.description: A single color stop with its position along the gradient axis, color, and bound variables if any

position.description: Value between 0 and 1 representing position along gradient axis.

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular gradient stop

Transform.description:
A transformation matrix is standard way in computer graphics to represent translation and rotation. These are the top two rows of a 3x3 matrix. The bottom row of the matrix is assumed to be [0, 0, 1]. This is known as an affine transform and is enough to represent translation, rotation, and skew.

The identity transform is [[1, 0, 0], [0, 1, 0]].

A translation matrix will typically look like:

```
[[1, 0, tx],
  [0, 1, ty]]
```

and a rotation matrix will typically look like:

```
[[cos(angle), sin(angle), 0],
  [-sin(angle), cos(angle), 0]]
```

Another way to think about this transform is as three vectors:

- The x axis (t[0][0], t[1][0])
- The y axis (t[0][1], t[1][1])
- The translation offset (t[0][2], t[1][2])

The most common usage of the Transform matrix is the `relativeTransform property`. This particular usage of the matrix has a few additional restrictions. The translation offset can take on any value but we do enforce that the axis vectors are unit vectors (i.e. have length 1). The axes are not required to be at 90° angles to each other.

ImageFilters.description: Image filters to apply to the node.

opacity.description: Overall opacity of paint (colors within the paint can also have opacity values which would blend with this)

blendMode.description: How this node blends with nodes behind it in the scene

type.description: The string literal "SOLID" representing the paint's type. Always check the `type` before reading other properties.

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular field on this paint

type.description: The string literal representing the paint's type. Always check the `type` before reading other properties.

gradientHandlePositions.description: This field contains three vectors, each of which are a position in normalized object space (normalized object space is if the top left corner of the bounding box of the object is (0, 0) and the bottom right is (1,1)). The first position corresponds to the start of the gradient (value 0 for the purposes of calculating gradient stops), the second position is the end of the gradient (value 1), and the third handle position determines the width of the gradient.

gradientStops.description: Positions of key points along the gradient axis with the colors anchored there. Colors along the gradient are interpolated smoothly between neighboring gradient stops.

type.description: The string literal "IMAGE" representing the paint's type. Always check the `type` before reading other properties.

imageRef.description: A reference to an image embedded in this node. To download the image using this reference, use the `GET file images` endpoint to retrieve the mapping from image references to image URLs.

imageTransform.description: Affine transform applied to the image, only present if `scaleMode` is `STRETCH`

scalingFactor.description: Amount image is scaled by in tiling, only present if scaleMode is `TILE`.

filters.description: Defines what image filters have been applied to this paint, if any. If this property is not defined, no filters have been applied.

gifRef.description: A reference to an animated GIF embedded in this node. To download the image using this reference, use the `GET file images` endpoint to retrieve the mapping from image references to image URLs.

type.description: The string literal "PATTERN" representing the paint's type. Always check the `type` before reading other properties.

sourceNodeId.description: The node id of the source node for the pattern

tileType.description: The tile type for the pattern

scalingFactor.description: The scaling factor for the pattern

horizontalAlignment.description: The horizontal alignment for the pattern

verticalAlignment.description: The vertical alignment for the pattern

LayoutConstraint.description: Layout constraint relative to containing Frame

vertical.description:
Vertical constraint (relative to containing frame) as an enum:

- `TOP`: Node is laid out relative to top of the containing frame
- `BOTTOM`: Node is laid out relative to bottom of the containing frame
- `CENTER`: Node is vertically centered relative to containing frame
- `TOP_BOTTOM`: Both top and bottom of node are constrained relative to containing frame (node stretches with frame)
- `SCALE`: Node scales vertically with containing frame

horizontal.description:
Horizontal constraint (relative to containing frame) as an enum:

- `LEFT`: Node is laid out relative to left of the containing frame
- `RIGHT`: Node is laid out relative to right of the containing frame
- `CENTER`: Node is horizontally centered relative to containing frame
- `LEFT_RIGHT`: Both left and right of node are constrained relative to containing frame (node stretches with frame)
- `SCALE`: Node scales horizontally with containing frame

Rectangle.description: A rectangle that expresses a bounding box in absolute coordinates.

x.description: X coordinate of top left corner of the rectangle.

y.description: Y coordinate of top left corner of the rectangle.

LayoutGrid.description: Guides to align and place objects within a frames.

pattern.description:
Orientation of the grid as a string enum

- `COLUMNS`: Vertical grid
- `ROWS`: Horizontal grid
- `GRID`: Square grid

sectionSize.description: Width of column grid or height of row grid or square grid spacing.

alignment.description:
Positioning of grid as a string enum

- `MIN`: Grid starts at the left or top of the frame
- `MAX`: Grid starts at the right or bottom of the frame
- `STRETCH`: Grid is stretched to fit the frame
- `CENTER`: Grid is center aligned

gutterSize.description: Spacing in between columns and rows

offset.description: Spacing before the first column or row

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular field on this layout grid

BaseShadowEffect.description: Base properties shared by all shadow effects

offset.description: How far the shadow is projected in the x and y directions

radius.description: Radius of the blur effect (applies to shadows as well)

spread.description:
The distance by which to expand (or contract) the shadow.

For drop shadows, a positive `spread` value creates a shadow larger than the node, whereas a negative value creates a shadow smaller than the node.

For inner shadows, a positive `spread` value contracts the shadow. Spread values are only accepted on rectangles and ellipses, or on frames, components, and instances with visible fill paints and `clipsContent` enabled. When left unspecified, the default value is 0.

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular field on this shadow effect

type.description: A string literal representing the effect's type. Always check the type before reading other properties.

showShadowBehindNode.description: Whether to show the shadow behind translucent or transparent pixels

type.description: A string literal representing the effect's type. Always check the type before reading other properties.

BaseBlurEffect.description: Base properties shared by all blur effects

type.description: A string literal representing the effect's type. Always check the type before reading other properties.

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular field on this blur effect

blurType.description: The string literal 'NORMAL' representing the blur type. Always check the blurType before reading other properties.
The string literal 'PROGRESSIVE' representing the blur type. Always check the blurType before reading other properties.

startRadius.description: The starting radius of the progressive blur

startOffset.description: The starting offset of the progressive blur

endOffset.description: The ending offset of the progressive blur

type.description: The string literal 'TEXTURE' representing the effect's type. Always check the type before reading other properties.

visible.description: Whether the texture effect is visible.

noiseSize.description: The size of the texture effect

radius.description: The radius of the texture effect

clipToShape.description: Whether the texture is clipped to the shape

noiseType.description: The string literal 'MONOTONE' representing the noise type.
The string literal 'MULTITONE' representing the noise type.

opacity.description: The opacity of the noise effect

noiseType.description: The string literal 'DUOTONE' representing the noise type.

secondaryColor.description: The secondary color of the noise effect

type.description: The string literal 'NOISE' representing the effect's type. Always check the type before reading other properties.

color.description: The color of the noise effect

visible.description: Whether the noise effect is visible.

blendMode.description: Blend mode of the noise effect

noiseSize.description: The size of the noise effect

density.description: The density of the noise effect

Style.description: A set of properties that can be applied to nodes and published. Styles for a property can be created in the corresponding property's panel while editing a file.

remote.description: Whether this style is a remote style that doesn't live in this file

EasingType.description:
This type is a string enum with the following possible values:

- `EASE_IN`: Ease in with an animation curve similar to CSS ease-in.
- `EASE_OUT`: Ease out with an animation curve similar to CSS ease-out.
- `EASE_IN_AND_OUT`: Ease in and then out with an animation curve similar to CSS ease-in-out.
- `LINEAR`: No easing, similar to CSS linear.
- `EASE_IN_BACK`: Ease in with an animation curve that moves past the initial keyframe's value and then accelerates as it reaches the end.
- `EASE_OUT_BACK`: Ease out with an animation curve that starts fast, then slows and goes past the ending keyframe's value.
- `EASE_IN_AND_OUT_BACK`: Ease in and then out with an animation curve that overshoots the initial keyframe's value, then accelerates quickly before it slows and overshoots the ending keyframes value.
- `CUSTOM_CUBIC_BEZIER`: User-defined cubic bezier curve.
- `GENTLE`: Gentle animation similar to react-spring.
- `QUICK`: Quick spring animation, great for toasts and notifications.
- `BOUNCY`: Bouncy spring, for delightful animations like a heart bounce.
- `SLOW`: Slow spring, useful as a steady, natural way to scale up fullscreen content.
- `CUSTOM_SPRING`: User-defined spring animation.

PaintOverride.description: Paint metadata to override default paints.

inheritFillStyleId.description: ID of style node, if any, that this inherits fill data from.

path.description: A series of path commands that encodes how to draw the path.

windingRule.description: The winding rule for the path (same as in SVGs). This determines whether a given point in space is inside or outside the path.

overrideID.description: If there is a per-region fill, this refers to an ID in the `fillOverrideTable`.

ArcData.description: Information about the arc properties of an ellipse. 0° is the x axis and increasing angles rotate clockwise.

startingAngle.description: Start of the sweep in radians.

endingAngle.description: End of the sweep in radians.

Hyperlink.description: A link to either a URL or another frame (node) in the document.

type.description: The type of hyperlink. Can be either `URL` or `NODE`.

url.description: The URL that the hyperlink points to, if `type` is `URL`.

nodeID.description: The ID of the node that the hyperlink points to, if `type` is `NODE`.

fontStyle.description: Describes visual weight or emphasis, such as Bold or Italic.

italic.description: Whether or not text is italicized.

textCase.description: Text casing applied to the node, default is the original casing.

textAlignHorizontal.description: Horizontal text alignment as string enum.

textAlignVertical.description: Vertical text alignment as string enum.

fills.description: An array of fill paints applied to the characters.

hyperlink.description: Link to a URL or frame.

opentypeFlags.description: A map of OpenType feature flags to 1 or 0, 1 if it is enabled and 0 if it is disabled. Note that some flags aren't reflected here. For example, SMCP (small caps) is still represented by the `textCase` field.

semanticWeight.description: Indicates how the font weight was overridden when there is a text style override.

semanticItalic.description: Indicates how the font style was overridden when there is a text style override.

paragraphSpacing.description: Space between paragraphs in px, 0 if not present.

paragraphIndent.description: Paragraph indentation in px, 0 if not present.

listSpacing.description: Space between list items in px, 0 if not present.

textDecoration.description: Text decoration applied to the node, default is none.

textAutoResize.description: Dimensions along which text will auto resize, default is that the text does not auto-resize. TRUNCATE means that the text will be shortened and trailing text will be replaced with "…" if the text contents is larger than the bounds. `TRUNCATE` as a return value is deprecated and will be removed in a future version. Read from `textTruncation` instead.

textTruncation.description: Whether this text node will truncate with an ellipsis when the text contents is larger than the text node.

maxLines.description: When `textTruncation: "ENDING"` is set, `maxLines` determines how many lines a text node can grow to before it truncates.

lineHeightPercent.description: Line height as a percentage of normal line height. This is deprecated; in a future version of the API only lineHeightPx and lineHeightPercentFontSize will be returned.

lineHeightPercentFontSize.description: Line height as a percentage of the font size. Only returned when `lineHeightPercent` (deprecated) is not 100.

lineHeightUnit.description: The unit of the line height value specified by the user.

isOverrideOverTextStyle.description: Whether or not this style has overrides over a text style. The possible fields to override are semanticWeight, semanticItalic, hyperlink, and textDecoration. If this is true, then those fields are overrides if present.

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular field on this style

allOf.description: Metadata for character formatting for styles used in text paths.

isOverrideOverTextStyle.description: Whether or not this style has overrides over a text style. The possible fields to override are semanticWeight, semanticItalic, and hyperlink. If this is true, then those fields are overrides if present.

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular field on this style

type.description: Type of node for this preferred value.

key.description: Key of this component or component set.

type.description: Type of this component property.

defaultValue.description: Initial value of this property for instances.

variantOptions.description: All possible values for this property. Only exists on VARIANT properties.

preferredValues.description: Preferred values for this property. Only applicable if type is `INSTANCE_SWAP`.

type.description: Type of this component property.

value.description: Value of the property for this component instance.

preferredValues.description: Preferred values for this property. Only applicable if type is `INSTANCE_SWAP`.

boundVariables.description: The variables bound to a particular field on this component property

Overrides.description: Fields directly overridden on an instance. Inherited overrides are not included.

id.description: A unique ID for a node.

ConnectorEndpoint.description: Stores canvas location for a connector start/end point.

endpointNodeId.description: Node ID that this endpoint attaches to.

position.description: The position of the endpoint relative to the node.

endpointNodeId.description: Node ID that this endpoint attaches to.

magnet.description: The magnet type is a string enum.

Component.description: A description of a main component. Helps you identify which component instances are attached to.

description.description: The description of the component as entered in the editor

componentSetId.description: The ID of the component set if the component belongs to one

documentationLinks.description: An array of documentation links attached to this component

remote.description: Whether this component is a remote component that doesn't live in this file

ComponentSet.description: A description of a component set, which is a node containing a set of variants of a component.

key.description: The key of the component set

description.description: The description of the component set as entered in the editor

documentationLinks.description: An array of documentation links attached to this component set

remote.description: Whether this component set is a remote component set that doesn't live in this file

DocumentationLink.description: Represents a link to documentation for a component or component set.

uri.description: Should be a valid URI (e.g. https://www.figma.com).

id.description: The id of the variable that the current variable is aliased to. This variable can be a local or remote variable, and both can be retrieved via the GET /v1/files/:file_key/variables/local endpoint.

Interaction.description: An interaction in the Figma viewer, containing a trigger and one or more actions.

trigger.description: The user event that initiates the interaction.

actions.description: The actions that are performed when the trigger is activated.

Trigger.description:
The `"ON_HOVER"` and `"ON_PRESS"` trigger types revert the navigation when the trigger is finished (the result is temporary). 
`"MOUSE_ENTER"`, `"MOUSE_LEAVE"`, `"MOUSE_UP"` and `"MOUSE_DOWN"` are permanent, one-way navigation.
The `delay` parameter requires the trigger to be held for a certain duration of time before the action occurs.
Both `timeout` and `delay` values are in milliseconds.
The `"ON_MEDIA_HIT"` and `"ON_MEDIA_END"` trigger types can only trigger from a video. 
They fire when a video reaches a certain time or ends. The `timestamp` value is in seconds.

deprecatedVersion.description:
Whether this is a [deprecated version](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/360040035834-Prototype-triggers#h_01HHN04REHJNP168R26P1CMP0A) of the trigger that was left unchanged for backwards compatibility.
If not present, the trigger is the latest version.

Action.description: An action that is performed when a trigger is activated.

OpenURLAction.description: An action that opens a URL.

UpdateMediaRuntimeAction.description: An action that affects a video node in the Figma viewer. For example, to play, pause, or skip.

oneOf.description:
An action that updates the runtime of a media node by playing, pausing, toggling play/pause, 
muting, unmuting, or toggling mute/unmute.

The `destinationId` is the node ID of the media node to update. If `destinationId` is `null`, the action will 
update the media node that contains the action.

The `mediaAction` is the action to perform on the media node.

An action that updates the runtime of a media node by skipping forward or backward.

The `destinationId` is the node ID of the media node to update. If `destinationId` is `null`, the action will 
update the media node that contains the action.

The `mediaAction` is the action to perform on the media node.

The `amountToSkip` is the amount of time to skip in seconds.

An action that updates the runtime of a media node by skipping to a specific time.

The `destinationId` is the node ID of the media node to update. If `destinationId` is `null`, the action will 
update the media node that contains the action.

The `mediaAction` is the action to perform on the media node.

The `newTimestamp` is the new time to skip to in seconds.

NodeAction.description: An action that navigates to a specific node in the Figma viewer.

preserveScrollPosition.description: Whether the scroll offsets of any scrollable elements in the current screen or overlay are preserved when navigating to the destination. This is applicable only if the layout of both the current frame and its destination are the same.

overlayRelativePosition.description: Applicable only when `navigation` is `"OVERLAY"` and the destination is a frame with `overlayPosition` equal to `"MANUAL"`. This value represents the offset by which the overlay is opened relative to this node.

resetVideoPosition.description: When true, all videos within the destination frame will reset their memorized playback position to 00:00 before starting to play.

resetScrollPosition.description: Whether the scroll offsets of any scrollable elements in the current screen or overlay reset when navigating to the destination. This is applicable only if the layout of both the current frame and its destination are the same.

resetInteractiveComponents.description: Whether the state of any interactive components in the current screen or overlay reset when navigating to the destination. This is applicable if there are interactive components in the destination frame.

Navigation.description:
The method of navigation. The possible values are:
- `"NAVIGATE"`: Replaces the current screen with the destination, also closing all overlays.
- `"OVERLAY"`: Opens the destination as an overlay on the current screen.
- `"SWAP"`: On an overlay, replaces the current (topmost) overlay with the destination. On a top-level frame, 
  behaves the same as `"NAVIGATE"` except that no entry is added to the navigation history.
- `"SCROLL_TO"`: Scrolls to the destination on the current screen.
- `"CHANGE_TO"`: Changes the closest ancestor instance of source node to the specified variant.

SimpleTransition.description: Describes an animation used when navigating in a prototype.

duration.description: The duration of the transition in milliseconds.

easing.description: The easing curve of the transition.

DirectionalTransition.description: Describes an animation used when navigating in a prototype.

duration.description: The duration of the transition in milliseconds.

easing.description: The easing curve of the transition.

matchLayers.description: When the transition `type` is `"SMART_ANIMATE"` or when `matchLayers` is `true`, then the transition will be performed using smart animate, which attempts to match corresponding layers an interpolate other properties during the animation.

type.description: The type of easing curve.

easingFunctionCubicBezier.description: A cubic bezier curve that defines the easing.

x1.description: The x component of the first control point.

y1.description: The y component of the first control point.

x2.description: The x component of the second control point.

y2.description: The y component of the second control point.

easingFunctionSpring.description: A spring function that defines the easing.

SetVariableAction.description: Sets a variable to a specific value.

SetVariableModeAction.description: Sets a variable to a specific mode.

ConditionalAction.description: Checks if a condition is met before performing certain actions by using an if/else conditional statement.

VariableData.description: A value to set a variable to during prototyping.

VariableDataType.description: Defines the types of data a VariableData object can hold

VariableResolvedDataType.description: Defines the types of data a VariableData object can eventually equal

Expression.description: Defines the [Expression](https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/articles/15253194385943) object, which contains a list of `VariableData` objects strung together by operators (`ExpressionFunction`).

ExpressionFunction.description: Defines the list of operators available to use in an Expression.

ConditionalBlock.description: Either the if or else conditional blocks. The if block contains a condition to check. If that condition is met then it will run those list of actions, else it will run the actions in the else block.

Measurement.description: A pinned distance between two nodes in Dev Mode

freeText.description: When manually overridden, the displayed value of the measurement

MeasurementStartEnd.description: The node and side a measurement is pinned to

MeasurementOffsetInner.description: Measurement offset relative to the inside of the start node

MeasurementOffsetOuter.description: Measurement offset relative to the outside of the start node

FrameOffset.description: Position of a comment relative to the frame to which it is attached.

node_offset.description: 2D vector offset within the frame from the top-left corner.

Region.description: Position of a region comment on the canvas.

region_height.description: The height of the comment region. Must be greater than 0.

region_width.description: The width of the comment region. Must be greater than 0.

comment_pin_corner.description: The corner of the comment region to pin to the node's corner as a string enum.

FrameOffsetRegion.description: Position of a region comment relative to the frame to which it is attached.

node_offset.description: 2D vector offset within the frame from the top-left corner.

region_height.description: The height of the comment region. Must be greater than 0.

region_width.description: The width of the comment region. Must be greater than 0.

comment_pin_corner.description: The corner of the comment region to pin to the node's corner as a string enum.

Comment.description: A comment or reply left by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for comment.

client_meta.description: Positioning information of the comment. Includes information on the location of the comment pin, which is either the absolute coordinates on the canvas or a relative offset within a frame. If the comment is a region, it will also contain the region height, width, and position of the anchor in regards to the region.

file_key.description: The file in which the comment lives

parent_id.description: If present, the id of the comment to which this is the reply

user.description: The user who left the comment

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the comment was left

resolved_at.description: If set, the UTC ISO 8601 time the comment was resolved

order_id.description: Only set for top level comments. The number displayed with the comment in the UI

reactions.description: An array of reactions to the comment

Reaction.description: A reaction left by a user.

user.description: The user who left the reaction.

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the reaction was left.

Emoji.description: The emoji type of reaction as shortcode (e.g. `:heart:`, `:+1::skin-tone-2:`). The list of accepted emoji shortcodes can be found in [this file](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/missive/emoji-mart/main/packages/emoji-mart-data/sets/14/native.json) under the top-level emojis and aliases fields, with optional skin tone modifiers when applicable.

id.description: Unique stable id of the user.

img_url.description: URL link to the user's profile image.

FrameInfo.description: Data on the frame a component resides in.

nodeId.description: The ID of the frame node within the file.

name.description: The name of the frame node.

backgroundColor.description: The background color of the frame node.

pageId.description: The ID of the page containing the frame node.

pageName.description: The name of the page containing the frame node.

nodeId.description: The ID of the state group node.

name.description: The name of the state group node.

containingComponentSet.description: The component set node that contains the frame node.

nodeId.description: The ID of the component set node.

name.description: The name of the component set node.

PublishedComponent.description: An arrangement of published UI elements that can be instantiated across figma files.

key.description: The unique identifier for the component.

file_key.description: The unique identifier of the Figma file that contains the component.

node_id.description: The unique identifier of the component node within the Figma file.

thumbnail_url.description: A URL to a thumbnail image of the component.

description.description: The description of the component as entered by the publisher.

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time when the component was created.

updated_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time when the component was last updated.

user.description: The user who last updated the component.

containing_frame.description: The containing frame of the component.

PublishedComponentSet.description: A node containing a set of variants of a component.

key.description: The unique identifier for the component set.

file_key.description: The unique identifier of the Figma file that contains the component set.

node_id.description: The unique identifier of the component set node within the Figma file.

thumbnail_url.description: A URL to a thumbnail image of the component set.

name.description: The name of the component set.

description.description: The description of the component set as entered by the publisher.

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time when the component set was created.

updated_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time when the component set was last updated.

user.description: The user who last updated the component set.

containing_frame.description: The containing frame of the component set.

PublishedStyle.description: A set of published properties that can be applied to nodes.

key.description: The unique identifier for the style

file_key.description: The unique identifier of the Figma file that contains the style.

node_id.description: ID of the style node within the figma file

thumbnail_url.description: A URL to a thumbnail image of the style.

description.description: The description of the style as entered by the publisher.

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time when the style was created.

updated_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time when the style was last updated.

user.description: The user who last updated the style.

sort_position.description: A user specified order number by which the style can be sorted.

Project.description: A Project can be identified by both the Project name, and the Project ID.

id.description: The ID of the project.
Unique identifier for version

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the version was created

label.description: The label given to the version in the editor

description.description: The description of the version as entered in the editor

user.description: The user that created the version

thumbnail_url.description: A URL to a thumbnail image of the file version.

WebhookV2.description: A description of an HTTP webhook (from Figma back to your application)

id.description: The ID of the webhook

event_type.description: The event this webhook triggers on

team_id.description: The team id you are subscribed to for updates. This is deprecated, use context and context_id instead

context.description: The type of context this webhook is attached to. The value will be "PROJECT", "TEAM", or "FILE"

context_id.description: The ID of the context this webhook is attached to

plan_api_id.description: The plan API ID of the team or organization where this webhook was created

status.description: The current status of the webhook

client_id.description: The client ID of the OAuth application that registered this webhook, if any

passcode.description: The passcode that will be passed back to the webhook endpoint. For security, when using the GET endpoints, the value is an empty string

endpoint.description: The endpoint that will be hit when the webhook is triggered

description.description: Optional user-provided description or name for the webhook. This is provided to help make maintaining a number of webhooks more convenient. Max length 140 characters.

WebhookV2Event.description: An enum representing the possible events that a webhook can subscribe to

WebhookV2Status.description:
An enum representing the possible statuses you can set a webhook to:
- `ACTIVE`: The webhook is healthy and receive all events
- `PAUSED`: The webhook is paused and will not receive any events

WebhookV2Request.description: Information regarding the most recent interactions sent to a webhook endpoint

webhook_id.description: The ID of the webhook the requests were sent to

error_msg.description: Error message for this request. NULL if no error occurred

WebhookV2RequestInfo.description: Information regarding the request sent to a webhook endpoint

id.description: The ID of the webhook

endpoint.description: The actual endpoint the request was sent to

payload.description: The contents of the request that was sent to the endpoint

sent_at.description: UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the request was sent

WebhookV2ResponseInfo.description: Information regarding the reply sent back from a webhook endpoint

status.description: HTTP status code of the response

received_at.description: UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the response was received

LibraryItemData.description: An object representing the library item information in the payload of the `LIBRARY_PUBLISH` event

key.description: Unique identifier for the library item

CommentFragment.description: An object representing a fragment of a comment left by a user, used in the payload of the `FILE_COMMENT` event. Note only ONE of the fields below will be set

text.description: Comment text that is set if a fragment is text based

mention.description: User id that is set if a fragment refers to a user mention

passcode.description: The passcode specified when the webhook was created, should match what was initially provided

timestamp.description: UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was triggered.

webhook_id.description: The id of the webhook that caused the callback

file_key.description: The key of the file that was updated

file_name.description: The name of the file that was updated

file_key.description: The key of the file that was deleted

file_name.description: The name of the file that was deleted

triggered_by.description: The user that deleted the file and triggered this event

created_at.description: UTC ISO 8601 timestamp of when the version was created

description.description: Description of the version in the version history

file_key.description: The key of the file that was updated

file_name.description: The name of the file that was updated

triggered_by.description: The user that created the named version and triggered this event

created_components.description: Components that were created by the library publish

created_styles.description: Styles that were created by the library publish

created_variables.description: Variables that were created by the library publish

modified_components.description: Components that were modified by the library publish

modified_styles.description: Styles that were modified by the library publish

modified_variables.description: Variables that were modified by the library publish

deleted_components.description: Components that were deleted by the library publish

deleted_styles.description: Styles that were deleted by the library publish

deleted_variables.description: Variables that were deleted by the library publish

file_key.description: The key of the file that was published

file_name.description: The name of the file that was published

library_item.description: The library item that was published

triggered_by.description: The user that published the library and triggered this event

created_at.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the comment was left

file_key.description: The key of the file that was commented on

file_name.description: The name of the file that was commented on

mentions.description: Users that were mentioned in the comment

triggered_by.description: The user that made the comment and triggered this event

file_key.description: The key of the file that was updated

file_name.description: The name of the file that was updated

node_id.description: The id of the node where the Dev Mode status changed. For example, "43:2"

related_links.description: An array of related links that have been applied to the layer in the file

status.description: The Dev Mode status. Either "NONE", "READY_FOR_DEV", or "COMPLETED"

triggered_by.description: The user that made the status change and triggered the event

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique stable id of the user.

email.description: Email associated with the user's account.

ActivityLogFileEntity.description: A Figma Design or FigJam file

type.description: The type of entity.

editor_type.description: Indicates if the object is a file on Figma Design or FigJam.

link_access.description: Access policy for users who have the link to the file.

proto_link_access.description: Access policy for users who have the link to the file's prototype.

ActivityLogFileRepoEntity.description: A file branch that diverges from and can be merged back into the main file

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the file branch.

ActivityLogProjectEntity.description: A project that a collection of Figma files are grouped under

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the project.

ActivityLogTeamEntity.description: A Figma team that contains multiple users and projects

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the team.

ActivityLogWorkspaceEntity.description: Part of the organizational hierarchy of managing files and users within Figma, only available on the Enterprise Plan

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the workspace.

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the organization.

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the plugin.

editor_type.description: Indicates if the object is a plugin is available on Figma Design or FigJam.

type.description: The type of entity.

id.description: Unique identifier of the widget.

editor_type.description: Indicates if the object is a widget available on Figma Design or FigJam.

ActivityLog.description: An event returned by the Activity Logs API.

id.description: The ID of the event.

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the event in seconds since the Unix epoch.

actor.description: The user who performed the action.

type.description: The type of the user.

id.description: The ID of the user.

name.description: The name of the user. For SCIM events, the value is "SCIM Provider". For official support actions, the value is "Figma Support".

action.description: The task or activity the actor performed.

type.description: The type of the action.

details.description: Metadata of the action. Each action type supports its own metadata attributes.

entity.description: The resource the actor took the action on. It can be a user, file, project or other resource types.

client_name.description: The third-party application that triggered the event, if applicable.

ip_address.description: The IP address from of the client that sent the event request.

is_figma_support_team_action.description: If Figma's Support team triggered the event. This is either true or false.

org_id.description: The id of the organization where the event took place.

team_id.description: The id of the team where the event took place -- if this took place in a specific team.

PaymentStatus.description: An object describing the user's payment status.

type.description:
The current payment status of the user on the resource, as a string enum:
  
- `UNPAID`: user has not paid for the resource
- `PAID`: user has an active purchase on the resource
- `TRIAL`: user is in the trial period for a subscription resource

PaymentInformation.description: An object describing a user's payment information for a plugin, widget, or Community file.

user_id.description: The ID of the user whose payment information was queried. Can be used to verify the validity of a response.

resource_id.description: The ID of the plugin, widget, or Community file that was queried. Can be used to verify the validity of a response.

date_of_purchase.description:
The UTC ISO 8601 timestamp indicating when the user purchased the resource. No value is given if the user has never purchased the resource.
  
Note that a value will still be returned if the user had purchased the resource, but no longer has active access to it (e.g. purchase refunded, subscription ended).

VariableScope.description:
Scopes allow a variable to be shown or hidden in the variable picker for various fields. This declutters the Figma UI if you have a large number of variables. Variable scopes are currently supported on `FLOAT`, `STRING`, and `COLOR` variables.

`ALL_SCOPES` is a special scope that means that the variable will be shown in the variable picker for all variable fields. If `ALL_SCOPES` is set, no additional scopes can be set.

`ALL_FILLS` is a special scope that means that the variable will be shown in the variable picker for all fill fields. If `ALL_FILLS` is set, no additional fill scopes can be set.

Valid scopes for `FLOAT` variables:
- `ALL_SCOPES`
- `TEXT_CONTENT`
- `WIDTH_HEIGHT`
- `GAP`
- `STROKE_FLOAT`
- `EFFECT_FLOAT`
- `OPACITY`
- `FONT_WEIGHT`
- `FONT_SIZE`
- `LINE_HEIGHT`
- `LETTER_SPACING`
- `PARAGRAPH_SPACING`
- `PARAGRAPH_INDENT`

Valid scopes for `STRING` variables:
- `ALL_SCOPES`
- `TEXT_CONTENT`
- `FONT_FAMILY`
- `FONT_STYLE`

Valid scopes for `COLOR` variables:
- `ALL_SCOPES`
- `ALL_FILLS`
- `FRAME_FILL`
- `SHAPE_FILL`
- `TEXT_FILL`
- `STROKE_COLOR`
- `EFFECT_COLOR`

VariableCodeSyntax.description: An object containing platform-specific code syntax definitions for a variable. All platforms are optional.

LocalVariableCollection.description: A grouping of related Variable objects each with the same modes.

id.description: The unique identifier of this variable collection.

name.description: The name of this variable collection.

key.description: The key of this variable collection.

modes.description: The modes of this variable collection.

modeId.description: The unique identifier of this mode.

parentModeId.description: The unique identifier of this mode's parent mode from the parent variable collection. This will be `undefined` if this mode does not inherit from a parent mode.

defaultModeId.description: The id of the default mode.

remote.description: Whether this variable collection is remote.

isExtension.description: Whether this variable collection is an extension of another variable collection.

parentVariableCollectionId.description: The id of the parent variable collection that this variable collection is an extension of. If this variable collection is not an extension, this value will be `undefined`.

variableOverrides.description: The overrides for the variables in this variable collection as a map of variable ids to a map of mode ids to variable values.

hiddenFromPublishing.description: Whether this variable collection is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.

variableIds.description: The ids of the variables in the collection. Note that the order of these variables is roughly the same as what is shown in Figma Design, however it does not account for groups. As a result, the order of these variables may not exactly reflect the exact ordering and grouping shown in the authoring UI.

LocalVariable.description: A Variable is a single design token that defines values for each of the modes in its VariableCollection. These values can be applied to various kinds of design properties.

id.description: The unique identifier of this variable.

variableCollectionId.description: The id of the variable collection that contains this variable.

resolvedType.description: The resolved type of the variable.

valuesByMode.description: The values for each mode of this variable.

hiddenFromPublishing.description:
Whether this variable is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.

If the parent `VariableCollection` is marked as `hiddenFromPublishing`, then this variable will also be hidden from publishing via the UI. `hiddenFromPublishing` is independently toggled for a variable and collection. However, both must be true for a given variable to be publishable.

scopes.description:
An array of scopes in the UI where this variable is shown. Setting this property will show/hide this variable in the variable picker UI for different fields.

Setting scopes for a variable does not prevent that variable from being bound in other scopes (for example, via the Plugin API). This only limits the variables that are shown in pickers within the Figma UI.

deletedButReferenced.description: Indicates that the variable was deleted in the editor, but the document may still contain references to the variable. References to the variable may exist through bound values or variable aliases.

PublishedVariableCollection.description: A grouping of related Variable objects each with the same modes.

id.description: The unique identifier of this variable collection.

subscribed_id.description: The ID of the variable collection that is used by subscribing files. This ID changes every time the variable collection is modified and published.

name.description: The name of this variable collection.

key.description: The key of this variable collection.

updatedAt.description:
The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the variable collection was last updated.

This timestamp will change any time a variable in the collection is changed.

PublishedVariable.description: A Variable is a single design token that defines values for each of the modes in its VariableCollection. These values can be applied to various kinds of design properties.

id.description: The unique identifier of this variable.

subscribed_id.description: The ID of the variable that is used by subscribing files. This ID changes every time the variable is modified and published.

variableCollectionId.description: The id of the variable collection that contains this variable.

resolvedDataType.description: The resolved type of the variable.

updatedAt.description: The UTC ISO 8601 time at which the variable was last updated.

VariableCollectionCreate.description: An object that contains details about creating a `VariableCollection`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable collection.

id.description: A temporary id for this variable collection.

name.description: The name of this variable collection.

initialModeId.description: The initial mode refers to the mode that is created by default. You can set a temporary id here, in order to reference this mode later in this request.

hiddenFromPublishing.description: Whether this variable collection is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.

parentVariableCollectionId.description: The id of the parent variable collection that this variable collection is extending from.

initialModeIdToParentModeIdMapping.description: Maps inherited modes from the parent variable collection to the initial mode ids on the extended variable collection.

VariableCollectionUpdate.description: An object that contains details about updating a `VariableCollection`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable collection.

id.description: The id of the variable collection to update.

name.description: The name of this variable collection.

hiddenFromPublishing.description: Whether this variable collection is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.

VariableCollectionDelete.description: An object that contains details about deleting a `VariableCollection`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable collection.

id.description: The id of the variable collection to delete.

VariableModeCreate.description: An object that contains details about creating a `VariableMode`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable mode.

id.description: A temporary id for this variable mode.

name.description: The name of this variable mode.

variableCollectionId.description: The variable collection that will contain the mode. You can use the temporary id of a variable collection. New modes cannot be created on extended collections.

VariableModeUpdate.description: An object that contains details about updating a `VariableMode`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable mode.

id.description: The id of the variable mode to update.

name.description: The name of this variable mode.

variableCollectionId.description: The variable collection that contains the mode. Modes cannot be updated on extended collections.

VariableModeDelete.description: An object that contains details about deleting a `VariableMode`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable mode.

id.description: The id of the variable mode to delete. Modes cannot be deleted on extended collections unless its parent mode has been deleted.

VariableCreate.description: An object that contains details about creating a `Variable`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable.

id.description: A temporary id for this variable.

variableCollectionId.description: The variable collection that will contain the variable. You can use the temporary id of a variable collection.

resolvedType.description: The resolved type of the variable.

hiddenFromPublishing.description: Whether this variable is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.

scopes.description: An array of scopes in the UI where this variable is shown. Setting this property will show/hide this variable in the variable picker UI for different fields.

VariableUpdate.description: An object that contains details about updating a `Variable`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable.

id.description: The id of the variable to update.

hiddenFromPublishing.description: Whether this variable is hidden when publishing the current file as a library.

scopes.description: An array of scopes in the UI where this variable is shown. Setting this property will show/hide this variable in the variable picker UI for different fields.

VariableDelete.description: An object that contains details about deleting a `Variable`.

action.description: The action to perform for the variable.

id.description: The id of the variable to delete.

VariableModeValue.description: An object that represents a value for a given mode of a variable. All properties are required.

variableId.description: The target variable. You can use the temporary id of a variable.

modeId.description: Must correspond to a mode in the variable collection that contains the target variable.

VariableValue.description: The value for the variable. The value must match the variable's type. If setting to a variable alias, the alias must resolve to this type. If overriding a value, the value type must match the variable's type. If removing an overridden value, the value must be `null`.

DevResource.description: A dev resource in a file

id.description: Unique identifier of the dev resource

name.description: The name of the dev resource.

url.description: The URL of the dev resource.

file_key.description: The file key where the dev resource belongs.

node_id.description: The target node to attach the dev resource to.
